steps.title: Find the slope from the difference condition
steps.content:
For a linear function $g(x) = mx + b$, the difference between consecutive values is constant and equals the slope:

$$g(x+1) - g(x) = [m(x+1) + b] - [mx + b] = m$$

Since we're told that $g(x+1) - g(x) = 6$, the slope is $m = 6$. Therefore, $g(x) = 6x + b$ for some unknown constant $b$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept using the point condition
steps.content:
We know $g(4) = 10$ and $g(x) = 6x + b$. Substitute:

$$6(4) + b = 10$$
$$24 + b = 10$$
$$b = -14$$

Therefore, $g(x) = 6x - 14$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
